title: Viewer live feed: SSE /kage/events from fs.watch on .agent_memory
summary: The local viewer server startViewer in mcp/daemon.ts serves GET /kage/events as text/event stream, backed by startLiveFeed projectRoot, { heartbeatMs, debounceMs } . The feed fs.watches .agent memory/packets new file pac
body:
The local viewer server (startViewer in mcp/daemon.ts) serves GET /kage/events as text/event-stream, backed by startLiveFeed(projectRoot, { heartbeatMs, debounceMs }). The feed fs.watches .agent_memory/packets (new file -> packet_written, rewrite -> packet_updated, title read from the packet JSON) and .agent_memory/reports for value.json appends (each new ledger entry -> value_event carrying the raw event). Duplicate fs.watch bursts are collapsed with a 100ms per-file debounce; clients get a ": connected" comment on attach and ": heartbeat" comments every 25s; close() tears down watchers, timers, and client responses. The viewer UI (mcp/viewer/console.js initLive) connects with EventSource and unhides the #livePanel on the Gains tab only after onopen — on static hosts with no daemon (GitHub Pages demo) the connection errors before opening and the panel stays hidden. The CSP connect-src 'self' already permits same-origin EventSource. Test seam: startLiveFeed is exported, so daemon.test.ts spins a plain node:http server around feed.handleRequest on port 0 and asserts streamed events after writing real files. Remember to bump the console.js?v=N cache-buster in index.html and mirror mcp/viewer/* to docs/viewer/* (kept as identical copies; CI pages.yml re-mirrors and diff-verifies).
Evidence: mcp/daemon.test.ts "live feed streams packet writes and value ledger events over SSE"; manual curl -N of /kage/events showed packet_written, value_event, packet_updated, heartbeat
Verified by: node --test dist/daemon.test.js (9 pass) and full suite 192 pass; browser verification of livePanel show/hide
